filter_index.pickup_city: Sheikh Feroze Tours & Travels, Sathu, Barbara Shah Chowk, Srinagar

recommended_months: Beginning of July to middle of September
avoid_months: All other seasons (outside July to mid-September)
snow_or_rain_notes: Snow remains at higher altitudes in early July; most snow melted by mid-July to third week of August; snowfall expected during the second week of September

Light evening showers in July; some evening showers mid-July to third week of August; rains reduce after end of August; Pir Panjal range blocks most rain clouds, making rains milder than Himachal Pradesh and Uttarakhand
temperature_notes: Early July: Day 15-20°C, Night 2-7°C; Mid-July to third week of August: Day 15-20°C, Night 4-8°C; Aug-end to second week of September: Day 14-18°C, Night 0-5°C; 3-4 warm layers in July to August, 4 warm layers in Aug-end to mid-September

terrain_challenges: Long trekking days, multiple days almost 10 km long
Last day is a 16 km stretch
Boulder sections begin on the way to Khaari campsite
Continuous boulder fields for over 3 km beyond Khaari
Descent to Patalwansar Lakes from Khari can be tricky
altitude_or_weather_risks: Highest altitude over 13,000 ft above sea level
Hit 10,000 ft mark on day 1 itself
Rapid elevation gain makes trekkers susceptible to Acute Mountain Sickness (AMS)
AMS can affect first-time and experienced trekkers alike; even fit persons climbing too high too soon are at equal risk
Chances of AMS are real above 10,000 ft

fitness_benchmark: Walk, jog, or run 5 km in under 32 minutes (minimum recommended fitness level)
approval_requirements: Fitness approval from the Indiahikes team is required 20 days before the trek date; without it, trekkers will not be allowed on the trek
At least 3 runs from the last 1 month must meet the trek's fitness criteria
Only running, jogging, or walking are accepted as fitness proofs; cross-training is not valid
prep_window: 6 to 8 weeks before the trek begins
A 6-week fitness plan is provided for those not currently active

experience_themes: Early July: snow at higher altitudes, trek accessible, colourful flowers appearing on meadows, lush green meadows, alpine lakes still frozen in parts, light evening showers
Mid-July to third week of August: most snow melted, frozen chunks in Patalwansar I & II melted, bright sunny mornings with some evening showers, green meadows densely dotted with wildflowers
Aug-end to second week of September: cold sets in, rains reduce, brilliant blue skies, deeper blue alpine lakes, meadows change colour from fresh green to a different tone, flowers change

Lush green meadows punctuated with alpine lakes still frozen in parts (early July); meadows densely dotted with wildflowers (mid-July to third week of August); meadows change colour from fresh green to a different tone, brilliant blue skies reflecting in deeper blue alpine lakes (Aug-end to mid-September)
Snow remains at higher altitudes in early July; most snow melted by mid-July to third week of August; snowfall expected during the second week of September
primary_watchouts: Trek window is tight; snowfall chances increase after second week of September, raising trek difficulty; crossing the pass becomes no longer possible; trail on the other side becomes inaccessible after second week of September
Trekkers visiting outside July to mid-September (trails buried under snow); trekkers after second week of September (pass crossing not possible, trail inaccessible)
Long trekking days, multiple days almost 10 km long
Last day is a 16 km stretch
Boulder sections begin on the way to Khaari campsite
Continuous boulder fields for over 3 km beyond Khaari
best_for: Trekkers seeking the only accessible window for Kashmir/Gurez Valley trails; those wanting wildflower meadows, alpine lakes, and changing seasonal landscapes
not_ideal_for: Trekkers visiting outside July to mid-September (trails buried under snow); trekkers after second week of September (pass crossing not possible, trail inaccessible)
Trekkers not prepared for rapid elevation gain and AMS risk
Trekkers without proper fitness and mental preparation for ascents, descents, and boulder sections
